width: 192px;"><h3 id="h_e0eb6be055">Images in topic viewpoints</h3></td><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880; width: 151px;"><p>png, jpg, jpeg</p></td><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880; width: 135px;"><p>Open<br/>W3C<br/>JPEG Group</p></td><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880;"><p><a class="intercom-content-link" href="https://support.catenda.com/en/articles/8053352-issue-body#h_fb43bacd99">Images can be attached</a> to the comment of a topic.<br/>360 degree images are displayed as panorama.</p></td></tr><tr><td style="width: 192px;"><h3 id="h_b7edcd751b">Images in topic viewpoints</h3></td><td style="width: 151px;"><p>gif (static image),</p></td><td style="width: 135px;"><p>Legacy, no governance</p></td><td><p><a class="intercom-content-link" href="https://support.catenda.com/en/articles/8430847-formatting-of-posts#h_65e9775fd0">Add links</a> to public images in the comment of text in a <a class="intercom-content-link" href="https://support.catenda.com/en/articles/8053352-issue-body#h_952e4743da">description</a> or <a class="intercom-content-link" href="https://support.catenda.com/en/articles/8053352-issue-body#h_f76b44d3ca">comment</a> of a topic.</p></td></tr><tr><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880; width: 192px;"><h3 id="h_d4cf4ca53b"><a class="intercom-content-link" href="#h_0306066baa">Images</a><br/><a class="intercom-content-link" href="#h_0306066baa">Vendor backed</a></h3></td><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880; width: 151px;"><p></p></td><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880; width: 135px;"><p></p></td><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880;"><p></p></td></tr><tr><td style="width: 192px;"><h3 id="h_a03f696bba">Open office Documents</h3></td><td style="width: 151px;"><p>odt, ods</p></td><td style="width: 135px;"><p>Open<br/>OASIS</p></td><td><p></p></td></tr><tr><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880; width: 192px;"><h3 id="h_9ac9be8d9f"><a class="intercom-content-link" href="#h_9bb0a3063c">Office</a><br/><a class="intercom-content-link" href="#h_9bb0a3063c">Vendor backed</a> </h3></td><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880; width: 151px;"><p></p></td><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880; width: 135px;"><p></p></td><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880;"><p></p></td></tr><tr><td style="width: 192px;"><h3 id="h_d51369e720">Rich text Documents</h3></td><td style="width: 151px;"><p>xml</p></td><td style="width: 135px;"><p>Open<br/>IETF/W3C/OASIS</p></td><td><p></p></td></tr><tr><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880; width: 192px;"><h3 id="h_dda4050762">Plain text Documents</h3></td><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880; width: 151px;"><p>txt</p></td><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880; width: 135px;"><p>Open<br/>W3C</p></td><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880;"><p></p></td></tr><tr><td style="width: 192px;"><h3 id="h_d3d9cecaed"><b>Comma Separated Value</b></h3></td><td style="width: 151px;"><p>csv</p></td><td style="width: 135px;"><p>Open<br/>IETF</p></td><td><p></p></td></tr></tbody></table></div>

## 4. **Vendor neutral multimedia file formats**

<div class="intercom-interblocks-table-container"><table role="presentation"><tbody><tr><td style="background-color: #e3e7fa80; width: 215px;"><h3 id="h_df4a2bf564"><b>Vendor backed Multimedia formats</b></h3></td><td style="background-color: #e3e7fa80; width: 112px;"><h3 id="h_e8919260c8">Extensions</h3></td><td style="background-color: #e3e7fa80;"><h3 id="h_7b016eb385">Standard type, Governance</h3></td><td style="background-color: #e3e7fa80;"><h3 id="h_527bed2c0c">Comments</h3></td></tr><tr><td style="width: 215px;"><h3 id="h_6ac728b57e">Xiph multimedia documents</h3></td><td style="width: 112px;"><p>ogg</p></td><td><p>Open<br/>IETF/Xiph.Org</p></td><td><p>Video, audio and other multimedia</p></td></tr><tr><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880; width: 215px;"><h3 id="h_265bfc961c"><a class="intercom-content-link" href="#h_c95f29d638">Multimedia</a><br/><a class="intercom-content-link" href="#h_c95f29d638">Vendor backed</a></h3></td><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880; width: 112px;"><p></p></td><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880;"><p></p></td><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880;"><p></p></td></tr></tbody></table></div>

## 5. **Vendor backed file formats**

There are a lot of file format standards developed by organizations that are used by many. It is important for us that it is easy to use Catenda. We therefore do our best to support as many of the most common filetypes that are used.

<div class="intercom-interblocks-table-container"><table role="presentation"><tbody><tr><td style="background-color: #e3e7fa80; width: 148px;"><h3 id="h_9d1dae63fd"><b>Vendor backed formats</b></h3></td><td style="background-color: #e3e7fa80; width: 115px;"><h3 id="h_96ef4bad49">Extensions</h3></td><td style="background-color: #e3e7fa80; width: 126px;"><h3 id="h_3819a0781c">Standard type, Governance</h3></td><td style="background-color: #e3e7fa80;"><h3 id="h_1c4db2987d">Comments</h3></td></tr><tr><td style="width: 148px;"><h3 id="h_353b8d88e6"><a class="intercom-content-link" href="#h_bb0f2e623a">PDF <br/>Vendor neutral</a></h3></td><td style="width: 115px;"><p></p></td><td style="width: 126px;"><p></p></td><td><p></p></td></tr><tr><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880; width: 148px;"><h2 id="h_f99f16e4e8">PDF Documents<i> </i></h2></td><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880; width: 115px;"><p>pdf <br/>(1.x to 2.0)<br/>xfdf</p></td><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880; width: 126px;"><p>Open<br/>IETF/Adobe</p></td><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880;"><p>Pdf comments will be visible when uploading a pdf. Only the cover page of a PDF portfolio will be displayed.</p></td></tr><tr><td style="width: 148px;"><h3 id="h_d0a7e72a6b">PDF Documents </h3></td><td style="width: 115px;"><p>fdf</p></td><td style="width: 126px;"><p>Proprietary<br/>Adobe</p></td><td><p></p></td></tr><tr><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880; width: 148px;"><h3 id="h_e9cbbf1a4d"><a class="intercom-content-link" href="#h_2297b41909">Images</a><br/><a class="intercom-content-link" href="#h_2297b41909">Vendor neutral</a></h3></td><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880; width: 115px;"><p></p></td><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880; width: 126px;"><p></p></td><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880;"><p></p></td></tr><tr><td style="width: 148px;"><h3 id="h_92afc0e808">Image Documents</h3></td><td style="width: 115px;"><p>bmp, wmf, emf</p></td><td style="width: 126px;"><p>Proprietary<br/>Microsoft</p></td><td><p></p></td></tr><tr><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880; width: 148px;"><h3 id="h_858be27ccd">Image Documents</h3></td><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880; width: 115px;"><p>tif, tiff</p></td><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880; width: 126px;"><p>Proprietary<br/>Adobe</p></td><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880;"><p></p></td></tr><tr><td style="width: 148px;"><h3 id="h_b2b5a34fd2">Images in topic descriptions and comments</h3></td><td style="width: 115px;"><p>bmp</p></td><td style="width: 126px;"><p>Proprietary<br/>Microsoft</p></td><td><p><a class="intercom-content-link" href="https://support.catenda.com/en/articles/8430847-formatting-of-posts#h_65e9775fd0">Add Links</a> to images in topic <a class="intercom-content-link" href="https://support.catenda.com/en/articles/8053352-issue-body#h_952e4743da">descriptions</a> or <a class="intercom-content-link" href="https://support.catenda.com/en/articles/8053352-issue-body#h_f76b44d3ca">comments</a>.<br/>360 degree images are displayed as panorama.</p></td></tr><tr><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880; width: 148px;"><h3 id="h_b9ef0d3ee7"><a class="intercom-content-link" href="#h_6b6ffc75b9">Multimedia</a><br/><a class="intercom-content-link" href="#h_6b6ffc75b9">Vendor netural</a></h3></td><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880; width: 115px;"><p></p></td><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880; width: 126px;"><p></p></td><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880;"><p></p></td></tr><tr><td style="width: 148px;"><h3 id="h_c95f29d638">Moving Picture Expert Group multimedia Documents</h3></td><td style="width: 115px;"><p>mp4</p></td><td style="width: 126px;"><p>Proprietary<br/>MPEG Group</p></td><td><p>Video, audio and other multimedia</p></td></tr><tr><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880; width: 148px;"><h3 id="h_6ede7be0f8">Autodesk Documents</h3></td><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880; width: 115px;"><p>dwg, dxf, dwf</p></td><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880; width: 126px;"><p>Proprietary<br/>Autodesk</p></td><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880;"><p>Vector graphics will be displayed with cad formats</p></td></tr><tr><td style="width: 148px;"><h3 id="h_d7857a1066">Bentley Documents</h3></td><td style="width: 115px;"><p>dgn</p></td><td style="width: 126px;"><p>Proprietary<br/>Bentley</p></td><td><p>Vector graphics will be displayed with cad formats</p></td></tr><tr><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880; width: 148px;"><h3 id="h_9e01ed9f53"><a class="intercom-content-link" href="#h_85b77d18aa">Office documents</a><br/><a class="intercom-content-link" href="#h_85b77d18aa">Vendor neutral</a></h3></td><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880; width: 115px;"><p></p></td><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880; width: 126px;"><p></p></td><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880;"><p></p></td></tr><tr><td style="width: 148px;"><h3 id="h_537a80bfe8">Microsoft documents</h3></td><td style="width: 115px;"><p>doc, xls, ppt, pub</p></td><td style="width: 126px;"><p>Proprietary<br/>Microsoft</p></td><td><p></p></td></tr><tr><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880; width: 148px;"><h3 id="h_1218a400a9">Microsoft Office Open XML<b> </b></h3></td><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880; width: 115px;"><p>docx, pptx, xlsx, xlsm</p></td><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880; width: 126px;"><p>Open<br/>Microsoft</p></td><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880;"><p>If there are macros in the xlsm the content cannot be previewed.</p></td></tr><tr><td style="width: 148px;"><h3 id="h_2026341a73"><b>Report templates</b></h3></td><td style="width: 115px;"><p>mrt</p></td><td style="width: 126px;"><p>Proprietary<br/>Stimulsoft</p></td><td><p>While the templates are stored using open technologies like XML and JSON, the specific schema, component definitions, and scripting logic are unique to the Stimulsoft ecosystem.</p></td></tr></tbody></table></div>

## 6. **Harmful file formats**

The following formats could be harmful when opened by just doubleclicked them. These filtypes cannot be uploaded with the single document upload or zip import features.

<div class="intercom-interblocks-table-container"><table role="presentation"><tbody><tr><td style="background-color: #e3e7fa80; width: 258px;"><h1 id="h_a12d12c72d"><b>Harmful formats</b></h1></td><td style="background-color: #e3e7fa80; width: 142px;"><h1 id="h_4b07086761">Extensions</h1></td><td style="background-color: #e3e7fa80;"><h1 id="h_5ce7154479">Comments</h1></td></tr><tr><td style="width: 258px;"><h3 id="h_bd332a1f35">Windows executables</h3></td><td style="width: 142px;"><p>exe</p></td><td><p></p></td></tr><tr><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880; width: 258px;"><h3 id="h_fad7bbd1ca">Windows installer packages</h3></td><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880; width: 142px;"><p>msi</p></td><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880;"><p></p></td></tr><tr><td style="width: 258px;"><h3 id="h_967ead787b">Batch scripts</h3></td><td style="width: 142px;"><p>bat</p></td><td><p></p></td></tr><tr><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880; width: 258px;"><h3 id="h_2d743a6fe1">Command scripts</h3></td><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880; width: 142px;"><p>cmd</p></td><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880;"><p></p></td></tr><tr><td style="width: 258px;"><h3 id="h_72ee4fd225">DOS executables</h3></td><td style="width: 142px;"><p>com</p></td><td><p></p></td></tr><tr><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880; width: 258px;"><h3 id="h_63c0a1e86b">Screensaver executables</h3></td><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880; width: 142px;"><p>scr</p></td><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880;"><p>Could be harmful if execute as program.</p></td></tr><tr><td style="width: 258px;"><h3 id="h_7143353393"><b>PowerShell scripts</b></h3></td><td style="width: 142px;"><p>ps1</p></td><td><p>Could be harmful if opened in a script host.</p></td></tr><tr><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880; width: 258px;"><h3 id="h_a9a3166afc"><b>Windows shortcuts</b></h3></td><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880; width: 142px;"><p>lnk</p></td><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880;"><p>Could be harmful when linking to an executable without looking like an executable.</p></td></tr><tr><td style="width: 258px;"><h3 id="h_61c6aaa719"><b>Java executable files</b></h3></td><td style="width: 142px;"><p>jar</p></td><td><p>Could be harmful if Java is installed.</p></td></tr><tr><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880; width: 258px;"><h3 id="h_59547abe10"><b>PHP Scripts</b></h3></td><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880; width: 142px;"><p>php</p></td><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880;"><p></p></td></tr></tbody></table></div>

# What file size can I upload?

The following table contains sizes up to which files can be uploaded: Files larger than these limits require alternative methods to be uploaded effectively.

The filesizes in the table below apply to individual files by themselves. Ther is no limit to the amount of files that there can be in a project.

<div class="intercom-interblocks-table-container"><table role="presentation"><tbody><tr><td style="background-color: #e3e7fa80; width: 173px;"><h1 id="h_dee0bdcb7c"><b>Upload size</b></h1></td><td style="background-color: #e3e7fa80; width: 108px;"><h3 id="h_644423a190"><b>Extension</b></h3></td><td style="background-color: #e3e7fa80;"><h3 class="intercom-align-center" id="h_0cc9638cff"><b>File size</b></h3></td></tr><tr><td style="width: 173px;"><h3 id="h_02ef636d2a">Document section</h3></td><td style="width: 108px;"><p>Any</p></td><td><p class="intercom-align-center">7 GB</p></td></tr><tr><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880; width: 173px;"><h3 id="h_79f0da432d">Ifc processing</h3></td><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880; width: 108px;"><p><code>.ifc</code></p></td><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880;"><p class="intercom-align-center">750 MB</p></td></tr><tr><td style="width: 173px;"><h3 id="h_7d57516cf7">IfcZIP processing</h3></td><td style="width: 108px;"><p><code>.ifczip</code></p></td><td><p class="intercom-align-center">750 MB</p></td></tr><tr><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880; width: 173px;"><h3 id="h_3c990303fb">Point Cloud</h3></td><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880; width: 108px;"><p><code>.e57</code> </p></td><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880;"><p class="intercom-align-center">25 GB</p></td></tr><tr><td style="width: 173px;"><h3 id="h_0bbd014cc0">Point Cloud</h3></td><td style="width: 108px;"><p><code>.las</code></p></td><td><p class="intercom-align-center">25 GB</p></td></tr><tr><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880; width: 173px;"><h3 id="h_788001d9cb">BCF Import</h3></td><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880; width: 108px;"><p><code>.bcf</code></p></td><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880;"><p class="intercom-align-center">500 MB</p></td></tr><tr><td style="width: 173px;"><h3 id="h_418d98af37">BCFZIP import</h3></td><td style="width: 108px;"><p><code>.bcfzip</code></p></td><td><p class="intercom-align-center">500 MB</p></td></tr></tbody></table></div>

## 1. **Splitting into smaller files**

Large .ifc and .ifczip files are recommended to be divided into smaller files. Splitting can help adhere to the 750 MB upload limitation and allows for more manageable file handling within projects.

By uploading several smaller files it both becomes easier for project members to turn on and off different parts and to download separate parts of the file individually.

## 2. **Exporting to IfcZip**

Some authoring tools support IfcZIP as an option when exporting IFC files.

> **Note:** **Example:** `3D-model.ifczip`

## 3. **Creating an IfcZip**

If the size of a `.ifc` exceeds the upper limit, a `.ifczip` file can be created to remain eligible for upload. Follow these steps to turn a `.ifc` into a `.ifczip`:

- Compress the `.ifc` file to a `.zip` file.
- Change the file extension from `.zip` to `.ifczip`

> **Note:** **Example:** `3D-model.ifc` v `3D-model.zip` v `3D-model.ifczip ​`

## 4. **Contact support**

If altering the file is not an option, and the file is larger than what can be uploaded, please let us know at [support@catenda.com](mailto:support@catenda.com). Files are handled on a case by case basis. Before reaching out, consider using alternative methods like creating an `.ifczip`, using the Desktop Connector, or splitting the file into smaller parts.

# Models as Documents

Previously it has been possible to upload IFC files to two separate places on Catenda Hub. With the _models as documents_ function these two locations for model files will be merged into one seamless function. If you make a model in the models section, a document will be linked and created in the documents section. If you upload an IFC document you can use the "make model" action button to link and create a model in the models section. With this function models in the models section can be handled like documents while models in the documents section can be handled just like models.

This article contains information about the following topics:

## 1. **Before/After migration - Main differences**

<div class="intercom-interblocks-table-container"><table role="presentation"><tbody><tr><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880; width: 126px;"><h1 id="h_3632d3cc1b"></h1></td><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880; width: 262px;"><h2 class="intercom-align-center" id="h_2093155130"><b>Before</b></h2></td><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880; width: 248px;"><h2 class="intercom-align-center" id="h_d5cfcbcb2d"><b>After</b></h2></td></tr><tr><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880; width: 126px;"><p class="intercom-align-right"><b>Main behavior</b></p></td><td style="width: 262px;"><p>Models existed only in the Model section. The user had to upload the same IFC file to both the Document and Model sections.</p></td><td style="width: 248px;"><p>Models are created from IFC files uploaded to the Document section, if the user requests it. The IFC file and its related model are then linked.</p></td></tr><tr><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880; width: 126px;"><p class="intercom-align-right"><b>User Interface</b></p></td><td style="width: 262px;"><p><b>Different</b> from the Document section and displayed less information, basically only a list of models.</p></td><td style="width: 248px;"><p><b>The same</b> as the Document section: a customisable table with related metadata.</p></td></tr><tr><td style="background-color: #e8e8e880; width: 126px;"><p class="intercom-align-right"><b>Access Rights</b></p></td><td style="width: 262px;"><p><b>Could not be applied</b> to models</p></td><td style="width: 248px;"><p><b>Can be applied</b> to models from their related document in the document section</p></td></tr></tbody></table></div>

## 2. **Familiar but different**

Now that the two sections have been linked it is important to note that there are still some key differences between the models and the documents sections. In the models section you will be able to see all model-documents gathered in a list. Here you will see your model-documents in a way that is similar to how they will be used in the 3D viewer. In the documents section you will be able to see document-models in your document structure. Here you will see your document-models in a way that is similar to how they will be used in your common data environment. All models are linked to each their own document and features from both sections can be used in both the models section and in the documents section.

## 3. **Models section changes**

With models as documents, the models section has changed in appearance. Instead of seeing menu items for each model they will now appear in a searchable table.

In the models section you will be able to find the following changes:

### 3.1 **Model table**

The new models table can look something like this:

![](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/catenda/help-center/main/images/dkw8jgdd/01-model-table.png)

### 3.2 **Columns**

The model list has columns which display most of the information you need to know about your models.

_Name_ The name of the model-document The model name is also the name you will see in the revision selector in the 3D view.

**Document name**

The name of the document-model in the documents section

**Revision name**

The name of the latest revision

### 3.3 **Access control**

If a model has been restricted for you in the documents section you neither see it in the documents section, the models table nor in the revision selector.

### 3.4 **Selecting table elements**

With the model table you can now select a range of models by holding shift. You can also add or remove models from your selection by holding control.

### 3.5 **Action buttons**

In the past, the only action that you could perform on selected models was the opening of these models in 3D. Now, you are able to download, remove and open the 2D view of your selected models. If you delete a model that is connected to a document, the document will loose the model connection, but the document will remain in the document section.

### 3.6 **Control access to document-models**

**Creating a model-document**

If you make a model with the create model button in the models section you will be asked to select where you want the linked document-model to end up in the documents-section. In the create model dialogue you will also be able to give the model a name. The resulting linked document-model will have the same as the model when it is created. Catenda Hub will remember the folder you chose last and will automatically select it the next time you create a model-document.

If your project began without models as documents, a folder called 'Models' will have appeared in your folder structure. The models folder that appears contains all document-models that are linked to model-documents in the model section. Document-models can be moved out of this folder to anywhere in the documents section you have access. Document-models in the Models folder can also be deleted (and restored) if desired. The document-models do not have to be in the folder and the models folder can be deleted if needed.

**Creating a model-revision**

To be able to upload new revisions to a model you now need at least write access to the document model. New revisions to the model can be added to the document and vice versa.

> **Note:** Revision comments have been disabled and can now optionally be enabled with [custom fields on revisions](https://support.catenda.com/en/articles/9531080-custom-fields-on-documents).

**Downloading a model-revision**

You need at least read access to the document-model to be able to download the model.

### 3.7 **Right information menu**

A right information menu will be available if a model is selected.

**Document field**

In this menu you will see your model information as well as a gray field that links to the document-model in the document section that is linked to this model-document. Click on the document field to open the document-model that is linked to this model.

**Model labels**

You can now also add labels to your models here.

**Model status**

If statuses have been configured in document settings you will be able to configure a status for your model here.

**Model transformation**

If you have opened this model in 3D, you will be able to configure model transformation here.

## 4. **Documents section changes**

While the visual changes are not as apparent as in the models section there are a few things that will change to the documents section when models as documents is activated. This is what the document-models can look like in the doucments section.

![](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/catenda/help-center/main/images/dkw8jgdd/02-documents-section-changes.png)

In the documents section you will be able to find the following changes:

### 4.1 **Model filter**

As soon as you have any models in the models section you will see a model filter appear in your filter menu. With this filter you can show/hide any document-models that have been created.

### 4.2 **Columns**

**Icon**

You will be able to tell apart a document-model from a regular document by the model badge on the bottom right of the document-model icon.

![](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/catenda/help-center/main/images/dkw8jgdd/03-columns.png)

**Name**

The name of the document

**Model name**

The name of the model If your ifc document has not been linked to a model you you will see a create model button here.

**Revision name**

The name of the latest revision in the model

**Viewer**

A column with buttons to open each individual document-model in the 3D viewer. Opening document-models in the 3D viewer is only possible if the document has been linked to a model.

### 4.3 **Action buttons**

Download, delete or load the 2D/3D views of selected models in the respective viewer by selecting one or more models.

<img alt="" src="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/catenda/help-center/main/images/dkw8jgdd/04-inline-group-a.png" width="310"/>    <img alt="" src="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/catenda/help-center/main/images/dkw8jgdd/05-inline-group-b.png" width="310"/>

This means that you can delete multiple models at once instead of one-by-one like before. If you delete a document that is connected to a model you will have to approve a warning that the model connected to the document will also be deleted.

> **Note:** This means you can delete a model without loosing the data. (Deleted documents can be recovered)

### 4.4 **Control access to model-documents**

**Creating document-models**

To be able to upload new revisions to a model you now need at least write access to the document model. You do this by creating a model in the action menu of a document. After you do this you will see the document as a model in the models section. The model-document in the models section will have the same name as the document model although these can each be changed later while they stay linked. New revisions to the model can be added as revisions to the document and vice versa.

> **Note:** This means you can create models from multiple IFC files at the same time instead of having to upload them one by one

**Uploading revisions to document-models**

You need at least write access to the document-model to be able to upload new revisions to the model. This means you can use the multi-upload feature to upload ifc files to multiple document-models at once

**Downloading document-models**

You need at least read access to the document-model to be able to download the model. This means that you can configure access to allow the downloading of separate models instead of just all or no models.

### 4.5 **Discoverability**

Document-models can now be found in the documents section like any other document.

- Document-models can be structured in folders to make it easier to navigate to the right set of models.
- Labels can be added to document-models to find all document-models that belong to one type.
- [Custom fields can be added to folders](https://support.catenda.com/en/articles/9531080-custom-fields-on-documents) where documents are uploaded to search on metadata-values related to each document-model
- [Custom fields can be added to folders](https://support.catenda.com/en/articles/9531080-custom-fields-on-documents) where documents are uplaoded to be able to add information to each revision in each document model.

See [here](https://support.catenda.com/en/articles/8542598-structuring-documents#h_7838a63c73) for suggestions on how to structure your document-models so they are easy to find.

### 4.6 **Approve shared IFC files**

IFC files can now be uploaded as shared revisions so they can go through an approval process before they are published.

### 4.7 **Naming convention with document-models**

Names in the documents section often include compressed abbreviations to keep the document name short while showing some information about of what this document is about. The name of the document-model can therefore be different from the name of the model-document to keep it in line with the other documents in the documents section while maintaining an easy to read name to use in the 3D viewer in the models section. The document name of the document-model will be the name that is recognized when uploading documents to the documents section. If the name is similar to or the same as the document, a new revision will automatically be created just like with other documents.

Because document-models behave the same as regular models it is now possible to use the naming convention with document-models to ensure that the participants of your project give the right name to the document upon uploading.

# Create, assign and manage topics

## 1. [Create, assign and manage topics](https://eu.guidde.com/playbooks/vRYbidpKvH49HzfhbKh8z6)

[Click here to watch](https://eu.guidde.com/share/playbooks/vRYbidpKvH49HzfhbKh8z6)

[Guidde video](https://embed.app.guidde.com/playbooks/eu_vRYbidpKvH49HzfhbKh8z6)

Learn how to create, assign, and manage topics on Catenda Hub.

### 1.1 Go to [hub.catenda.com](https://hub.catenda.com/)

### 1.2 Create a topic via the "+" button at the top right

From the list of topics of your choice, create a new Topic by clicking on the "+" button at the top right.

![Create a topic via the '+' button at the top right](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/catenda/help-center/main/images/mmrex0mx/01-1-create-a-topic-via-the-button-at-the-top-right.png)

### 1.3 Enter the title of your topic here.

Enter the title of your Subject here.

![Enter the title of your topic here.](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/catenda/help-center/main/images/mmrex0mx/02-2-enter-the-title-of-your-topic-here.png)

### 1.4 Set the status and type of the subject via the drop-down menus.

Set the status and type of the Subject via the drop-down menus.

![Set the status and type of the subject via the drop-down menus.](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/catenda/help-center/main/images/mmrex0mx/03-3-set-the-status-and-type-of-the-subject-via-the-drop-down-menus.png)

### 1.5 Enter a due date here.

Enter a due date here.

![Enter a due date here.](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/catenda/help-center/main/images/mmrex0mx/04-4-enter-a-due-date-here.png)

### 1.6 Assign the topic to a user or team here.

Assign the Topic to a user or team here.

![Assign the topic to a user or team here.](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/catenda/help-center/main/images/mmrex0mx/05-5-assign-the-topic-to-a-user-or-team-here.png)

### 1.7 Add tags here that will allow you to filter and easily find your topic.

Add tags here that will allow you to filter and easily find your Topic.

![Add tags here that will allow you to filter and easily find your topic.](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/catenda/help-center/main/images/mmrex0mx/06-6-add-tags-here-that-will-allow-you-to-filter-and-easily-find-your-topic.png)

### 1.8 Fill in custom fields set up by your project administrator here.

Fill in custom fields set up by your project administrator here.

![Fill in custom fields set up by your project administrator here.](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/catenda/help-center/main/images/mmrex0mx/07-7-fill-in-custom-fields-set-up-by-your-project-administrator-here.png)

### 1.9 Add a description here.

Add a description here.

![Add a description here.](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/catenda/help-center/main/images/mmrex0mx/08-8-add-a-description-here.png)

### 1.10 Add a comment here.

Add a comment here.

![Add a comment here.](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/catenda/help-center/main/images/mmrex0mx/09-9-add-a-comment-here.png)

### 1.11 You can now create your Topic by clicking on the send button.

You can now create your Topic by clicking on the Send button

![You can now create your Topic by clicking on the send button.](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/catenda/help-center/main/images/mmrex0mx/10-10-you-can-now-create-your-topic-by-clicking-on-the-send-button.png)

### 1.12 Find your topic in its list.

Then find your saved Topic in the previously selected list.

![Find your topic in its list.](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/catenda/help-center/main/images/mmrex0mx/11-11-find-your-topic-in-its-list.png)

### 1.13 Manage, sort and filter topics in your list.

You now have the ability to filter and sort the different topics in your list. They can be sorted using the fully configurable table: by type, status, label, due date, last topic created, assigned person, etc. You can also filter topics from the filter panel that can be activated on the left. Here you can filter topics in the same way as for sorting topics, and thus isolate for example topics with a specific status.

![Manage, sort and filter topics in your list.](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/catenda/help-center/main/images/mmrex0mx/12-12-manage-sort-and-filter-topics-in-your-list.png)

### 1.14 You can also create a topic directly from a 3D view.

You can also create a Topic directly from a 3D view. To do this, keep the 3D window open, then create a Topic via the "+" button.

![You can also create a topic directly from a 3D view.](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/catenda/help-center/main/images/mmrex0mx/13-13-you-can-also-create-a-topic-directly-from-a-3d-view.png)

### 1.15 Fill in the various fields.

Fill in the different fields here, in the same way as seen previously.

![Fill in the various fields.](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/catenda/help-center/main/images/mmrex0mx/14-14-fill-in-the-various-fields.png)

### 1.16 From a 3D view, you also have the possibility to add a marker on the associated 2D view.

From a 3D view, you also have the possibility to add a marker on the associated 2-D view.

![From a 3D view, you also have the possibility to add a marker on the associated 2D view.](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/catenda/help-center/main/images/mmrex0mx/15-15-from-a-3d-view-you-also-have-the-possibility-to-add-a-marker-on-the-associated-2d-view.png)

### 1.17 Select the location of the Subject on the 2D view.

Select the location of the Subject on the 2D view.

![Select the location of the Subject on the 2D view.](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/catenda/help-center/main/images/mmrex0mx/16-16-select-the-location-of-the-subject-on-the-2d-view.png)

### 1.18 Finally, you also have the option to associate the current view with the Subject being created.

Finally, you also have the option to associate the current view with the Subject being created.

![Finally, you also have the option to associate the current view with the Subject being created.](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/catenda/help-center/main/images/mmrex0mx/17-17-finally-you-also-have-the-option-to-associate-the-current-view-with-the-subject-being-created.png)

### 1.19 To complete the creation of the Subject, click on send.

To complete the Topic creation, click Submit. You will always have the option to return to this saved view attached to this Topic.

![To complete the creation of the Subject, click on send.](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/catenda/help-center/main/images/mmrex0mx/18-18-to-complete-the-creation-of-the-subject-click-on-send.png)

This guide showed you how to create, assign and manage Topics in Catenda Hub, from a Topic list or a 3D view.

# Report templates page

> **Note:** **Note 1:** The reports page is an opt-in feature that can be requested to be enabled for ongoing projects. **Note 2:** New projects that are created based on a template project where this feature is enabled do not have this feature enabled.

The report templates page can be found as a sub-page of the [reports page](https://support.catenda.com/en/articles/12303098-reports-page) in the left navigation menu. On the report templates page document and topic templates that are available to the project can be managed. _Access required:_ Administrator

This is what the report templates page can look something like this:

![](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/catenda/help-center/main/images/5aw6t2k2/01-intro.png)

This article contains information about the following topics [Actions](#h_56ff8f93e0) - [Filter by name](#h_b78962b6dc) - [Report templates table](#h_4a5c2d4a75)

## 1. **Actions on the report templates page**

The report templates page actions can be found by clicking on the action menu on the top right of the report templates page. This is what the report templates page actions can look like:

![](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/catenda/help-center/main/images/5aw6t2k2/02-actions-on-the-report-templates-page.png)

### 1.1 **New topic template**

Use the new topic template action to create a new topic template

### 1.2 **New document template**

Use the new document template action to create a new document template

## 2. **Filter by name**

Write in the search bar to filter the reports template table by the names of the reports.

![](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/catenda/help-center/main/images/5aw6t2k2/03-filter-by-name.png)

Text searc - `search=test`

## 3. **Report templates table**

As soon as a report template is created it will show up as a row in the report templates table. This is what the report templates table can look like:

![](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/catenda/help-center/main/images/5aw6t2k2/04-report-templates-table.png)

Click [here](https://support.catenda.com/en/articles/11748020-tables-on-catenda) to read more about how to work with tables in Catenda.

### 3.1 **Row content**

**Report row**

While it is possible to click on a row to select it and highlight it in green there are no actions that can be performed by selecting a row. The row will be selected when the row action menu in the right column is selected.

### 3.2 **Columns**

Based on the configured column order the first columns are displayed while the table might have to be scrolled sideways to show other enabled columns. The default order and visibility setting of the columns on the report templates page is as follows:

Name - _Default_ The name of the report template

_Created by_ - _Default_ The creator of the report template

Created - _Default_ The time and date of when the topic template was created

Type - Default The type of report template, either Documents or Topics.

Action menu - Default Click on the action menu in the action menu column to open the row action menu. This is what the report template row action menu can look like:

![](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/catenda/help-center/main/images/5aw6t2k2/05-columns.png)

Edit Use the edit action to edit the selected report template.

Download Use the download action to download the selected report template with the `.mrt` file format that can be imported again in any other project.

Delete Use the delete action to delete the selected report template.
